item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A remains moderately society-leaning throughout, repeatedly rejecting Agent B's constitutional individual-rights framing while allowing that Indonesian constitutional freedom exists with limits. Agent B remains rights-leaning throughout, allowing only narrow public-safety limits and repeatedly grounding the stance in U.S. constitutional principles. Both agents engage the other side's legal framing, but no clear flip or strong net convergence is visible in the text.
